summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js with install-time host fingerprinting and outbound POST
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under a fake @solana-labs scope. The package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that auto-executes install.js on `npm install`. install.js imports `os`, `fs`, `http`, `https`, and child_process; it collects host identifiers including `os.hostname()` and `process.platform`, invokes `execSync` and shells out to `curl`, and performs an outbound HTTPS POST. The combination of brand-impersonation naming, install-time auto-execution, host fingerprint collection, and outbound network beaconing is the standard supply-chain credential/host-enumeration attack shape; installers running `npm install @solana-labs/web3js` will execute this code automatically and leak host data off-machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
